page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture cloud server powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. This instance features 4 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 4 vCPUs. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server is deployed on the KVM hypervisor and includes 959 GB of bundled local SSD storage.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Because it bundles a substantial 959 GB local SSD with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, the ecs.i4.xlarge offers high resource density for storage-intensive and memory-intensive workloads. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring direct access to fast local storage without the overhead of detached network storage. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, NoSQL databases, caching layers, and low-latency data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 4 vCPUs alongside 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance is equipped with 959 GB of bundled local SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The integration of local SSD storage and dedicated CPU resources makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring fast local data access and consistent compute performance. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable execution times, while the local SSD eliminates network latency for storage operations. It is designed for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-intensive applications.
